assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: '| Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5/5 criteria met |'. Each individual criterion file confirms PASS: criterion-1.md (Single License Filter - PASS), criterion-2.md (Comma-Separated License Filter - PASS), criterion-3.md (400 Bad Request for Invalid License - PASS), criterion-4.md (Pagination Integration - PASS), criterion-5.md (Unchanged Response Shape - PASS).

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: '| Scope Containment | PASS | All files in diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) listed in task's Files to Modify/Create |'. The Intent Alignment section (lines 23-29) provides file-by-file confirmation: list.rs 'Listed under Files to Modify', service/mod.rs 'Listed under Files to Modify', tests/api/package.rs 'Listed under Files to Create'. Line 29: 'No files outside the task specification were modified.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR |'. Line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created |'. No output files reference sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis/report files: report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. These files contain code snippets quoted from the diff for analysis purposes only (e.g., criterion-1.md quotes 'pub struct PackageListParams' as evidence). No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) appear in the outputs. The report presents findings without any indication of code modification.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' (line 17) followed by detailed findings sections. No merge command, merge action, or merge recommendation appears anywhere in the outputs. The report format (verification table with checks/results/details, detailed findings per domain) is structured for human consumption and decision-making.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: '| Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No passwords, API keys, or private keys found |'. The Security section (lines 33-40) provides detailed scanning results: 'No hardcoded passwords, API keys, secrets, or private keys detected', 'No .env files, credential files, or token strings present in the diff', 'No use of unsafe blocks or raw SQL that could introduce injection vectors'.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test file tests/api/package.rs added |'. The Style/Conventions section (lines 64-66) confirms: 'Test change classification: ADDITIVE' and 'Only a new test file tests/api/package.rs was added. No existing test files were modified or removed.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: The report summary table contains all listed verdicts: Scope Containment (line 7, PASS), Diff Size (line 8, PASS), Commit Traceability (line 9, N/A) — all under Intent Alignment section (line 21); Sensitive Patterns (line 10, PASS) — under Security section (line 31); CI Status (line 11, PASS), Acceptance Criteria (line 12, PASS), Verification Commands (line 15, N/A) — under Correctness section (line 43); Test Quality (line 13, PASS with 'Eval Quality: N/A') and Test Change Classification (line 14, ADDITIVE) — under Style/Conventions section (line 58). All four domain sections are present with their respective verdicts.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 23-29): file-by-file scope comparison listing all 3 files with specific descriptions of changes in each. Security (lines 33-40): 5 specific line-level scanning findings including SQL injection analysis ('parameterized SeaORM query methods') and input validation assessment. Correctness (lines 47-55): per-criterion code-level verification for all 5 criteria with specific code references (e.g., 'package_license::Column::License.is_in(licenses)' for criterion 1). Style/Conventions (lines 59-66): test quality assessment citing specific patterns ('test_context(TestContext)', 'Given/When/Then comments', coverage of all four test requirements).

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | PASS | Well-structured additive tests; Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews) |'. Eval Quality is explicitly marked N/A with the reason '(no eval result reviews)'. Test Quality is PASS despite Eval Quality being N/A, confirming it does not affect the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 13: 'Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews)'). report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created |'. No output files reference sub-task creation of any kind, including eval failure sub-tasks.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR |'. Line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created |'. These two verdicts appear in the summary table (lines 5-6) but do not appear under any of the four domain sections (Intent Alignment line 21, Security line 31, Correctness line 43, Style/Conventions line 58), confirming they are determined independently from domain analysis.
